supply_chain.dependencies: Hydrocarbon-based raw materials (ethane, propane, butane, naphtha, condensate) derived from natural gas liquids (NGLs) and crude oil production. Primary feedstocks for ethylene and propylene production. Reliance on natural gas for electricity generation.
supply_chain.risks: Supply chain disruptions from industry capacity constraints, material availability, global logistics delays, third-party service and material providers. Transportation capacity constraints (ocean shipping containers). Global labor availability constraints. Disruptions from tenant and neighboring manufacturing operations. Geopolitical tensions (Russia-Ukraine conflict, Middle East conflicts) causing supply chain and logistics disruptions, volatility in feedstock/energy prices (especially in Europe). Severe weather events and natural disasters disrupting operations. Cyberattacks and cybersecurity incidents. Concentration risk in certain geographic regions for manufacturing.

Use of contract manufacturers for assembly and printed circuit board production. Dependence on contract manufacturers may adversely affect ability to bring solutions to market. Inability to adjust purchases due to changing market conditions could adversely affect income. Concentration risk if suppliers cease manufacturing components requiring re-engineering of solutions.

supply_chain.dependencies: The Company uses many different raw materials, most of which are commodities. Raw materials include wheat, corn, oats, various vegetables, vegetable oils, beef, pork, poultry, dairy products, steel, aluminum, and energy. The Company procures a wide spectrum of commodities globally.
supply_chain.risks: Supply chain disruptions have in the past and could continue to negatively impact profitability. Disruptions include transportation issues, labor challenges, global economic conditions, inflation, rising interest rates, decreased availability of capital, volatility in financial markets, declining consumer spending rates, recessions, decreased energy availability and increased energy costs (including fuel surcharges). Disruptions or inefficiencies in supply chain and/or operations. Risks related to the availability and prices of commodities and other supply chain resources, including raw materials, packaging, energy, and transportation, weather conditions, health pandemics or outbreaks of disease, or other geopolitical uncertainty. Dependency on contract manufacturing arrangements could impact sales volume and adversely affect results of operations. Operations are dependent on a wide array of third parties including suppliers, contract manufacturers, third-party outsourcers, warehousing partners, and transportation providers.

supply_chain.dependencies: We depend on suppliers to provide equipment to operate our business and satisfy customer demand, including network equipment, customer premises equipment and wireless-related equipment such as mobile hotspots, handsets, wirelessly enabled computers, wireless data cards and other connected devices. In some instances, we depend on key single-source suppliers to provide important inputs where there are few alternative suppliers available.
supply_chain.risks: These suppliers could fail to provide equipment on a timely or cost-effective basis, or fail to meet our performance expectations, for a number of reasons, including difficulties in obtaining export licenses for certain technologies, inflationary pressures, inability to secure component parts, general business disruption, natural disasters, safety issues, economic and political instability, including the outbreak of war and other hostilities, and public health emergencies. In certain limited circumstances, suppliers have been unable to supply products in a timely fashion, affecting our ability to provide products and services precisely as and when requested by our customers.

sanction_risks: Involvement with foreign firms exposes us to the risk of being unable to control the actions of those firms and therefore exposes us to risks associated with our obligation to comply with the Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA)

supply_chain.dependencies: Sheepskin used to manufacture a significant portion of UGG brand products is sourced primarily from designated suppliers in Australia and processed by two tanneries in China. Less than 5% of finished goods for footwear comes from China while the remainder comes from Southeast Asia, predominately from Vietnam.
supply_chain.risks: Supply chain disruptions could interrupt product manufacturing and global logistics and increase product and transportation costs. Port congestion, temporary closures, vessel availability and reliability, worker shortages, government-imposed restrictions, such as border closures and shipment restrictions, as well as trade policy changes, may disrupt operations. Geopolitical conflicts have and could in the future bring about disruption and volatility in global markets, supply chains, and logistics. Elevated inventory levels, combined with uneven flow of receipts and shipments, could cause capacity pressures within US warehouses and DCs and international 3PLs. Labor disputes that affect partners create significant risk, particularly if such disputes result in work slowdowns, strikes, or other disruptions.

supply_chain.dependencies: Glass bottles are the largest cost component of packaging materials. The Glass Plant (joint venture with Owens-Illinois) supplies nearly 60% of total annual glass bottle supply for Mexican beer brands. One producer supplies most glass container requirements for U.S. operations. Two aluminum can suppliers provide all requirements for Mexican beer brands, with one supplier providing majority. Limited number of glass producers in U.S. industry. Dependent on water supplies from separate aquifers for Mexican breweries. Dependent on grapes from approximately 350 independent growers in U.S. and 35 in New Zealand and Italy.
supply_chain.risks: Concentration risk with limited glass bottle suppliers. Supply chain disruptions from tariffs, geopolitical events, weather events (wildfires, droughts, floods in California and Oregon, severe winter storms). Water scarcity risks in California and Mexico. Dependence on agricultural products subject to weather, diseases, pests. Single-source dependencies for certain packaging materials. Rail and freight shipping disruptions. Border closure risks between U.S. and Mexico. Capacity constraints if Mexican breweries disrupted.

supply_chain.dependencies: DGD sources waste and renewable feedstocks (predominantly animal fats, used cooking oils, vegetable oils, and inedible distillers corn oils) from suppliers throughout the world. Darling supplies some of DGD's waste feedstock at competitive pricing under a raw material supply agreement, but DGD must secure a significant amount of its waste and renewable feedstock requirements from other sources. Ethanol segment relies on corn sourced from local farmers and commercial elevators in the Mid-Continent region of the U.S.
supply_chain.risks: If one or more supply contracts were terminated, or if political or other events were to disrupt traditional feedstock and other critical supplies, adequate alternative supplies may not be available or may only be available at increased prices or costs. Refineries and plants without access to waterborne deliveries or offtake must rely on rail, pipeline, or ground transportation and are more susceptible to supply disruption risks. DGD has increasingly sourced feedstocks from international sources due to increased production of renewable diesel and competition for feedstocks, intensifying exposure to political, geographic, regulatory, tax, and economic risks. Ethanol supply is exposed to weather and environmental events in the Mid-Continent region affecting crop production.

supply_chain.dependencies: We host our Cloud-based applications and serve our customers from facilities operated by third-party providers, primarily Microsoft Azure. We rely on third party vendors to initiate payments on behalf of our customers. We depend on strategic relationships with third parties who provide us with services including connected payroll partners, implementation partners, systems integrators, third party sales channel partners, the operators of data centers, and banks and other providers who execute wire transfers and other money movement services.
supply_chain.risks: Disruptions in the financial markets can adversely affect our lenders, insurers, customers, and other counterparties. If our financial counterparties are unable to fund borrowings under their revolving credit commitments or investments under their Receivables Purchase Agreement, or we are unable to borrow or refinance our debt in the financial markets, it could substantially increase our cost of borrowing. Any disruption or delay to data flow in critical time periods could lead to the disruption of fund movement. We rely on third-party service providers to perform services in certain geographies where we do not currently have international operations. We have experienced, and may in the future experience, disruptions, outages, and other performance problems caused by hardware failures, infrastructure changes, human or software errors, viruses, security attacks, fraud, increased resource consumption, denial of service attacks and Cloud interruptions run by third party service providers.

supply_chain.dependencies: Most raw materials and energy used in production are purchased from outside sources. Raw materials include both organic, primarily petroleum-derived, materials and inorganic materials, including titanium dioxide. Supply of critical raw materials is managed by establishing contracts with multiple sources and identifying alternative materials or technology whenever possible.
supply_chain.risks: Raw material supply chain disruptions, including logistical and transportation challenges, could adversely impact ability to procure raw materials. An inability to obtain certain critical raw materials has adversely impacted ability to produce certain products in the past. Raw material costs fluctuate due to changes in supplier feedstock costs and inventories, global industry activity levels, foreign currency exchange rates, government regulation, tariffs, and global supply and demand factors.

supply_chain.dependencies: Some raw materials are purchased from a limited set of suppliers, including international sources. Some items purchased for manufacture are from limited or single sources of supply due to technical capability, price, and other factors. Sole source supply is more common among businesses heavily involved in research and development. China is a key supplier for certain materials including gallium and germanium used in semiconductor manufacturing.
supply_chain.risks: Disruption of supply sources or supplier-imposed rationing of scarce components could cause delays or reductions in shipments. International sources pose additional risks including political instability, export restrictions, and currency fluctuations. Component and raw material shortages have impacted ability to manufacture and ship products. Supply chain constraints resulted in sustained increases in prices for components and raw materials. China has unveiled restrictions on exports of gallium and germanium which has impacted production and pricing of digital imaging products. Counterfeit parts risk could be exacerbated by supply chain shortages or parts becoming obsolete.

supply_chain.dependencies: The Company is currently dependent on Boeing as the sole manufacturer of the Company's aircraft. The Company operates an all-Boeing fleet, all of which are variations of the Boeing 737. Boeing no longer manufactures versions of the 737 other than the MAX family of aircraft. The Company is also dependent on sole or limited suppliers for aircraft engines and certain other aircraft parts and services.
supply_chain.risks: Boeing has in the past experienced, and may continue to experience, delays in fulfilling its commitments with regards to delivery of the Boeing 737-8 to the Company as a result of manufacturing challenges. The Company's contractual delivery schedule for the Boeing 737-7 is dependent on the FAA issuing required certifications and approvals to Boeing and the Company. If the MAX aircraft were to become unavailable for the Company's flight operations, the Company's operations would be materially adversely affected. The Company is also dependent on sole or limited suppliers for aircraft engines and certain other aircraft parts and services, and could be materially adversely affected if unable to obtain timely or sufficient delivery of aircraft parts or equipment or adequate maintenance or other support from any of these suppliers at commercially reasonable terms, if suppliers were unable to achieve and/or maintain required regulatory certifications or approvals of their parts or equipment, or in the event of a mechanical or regulatory issue associated with the Company's aircraft parts or equipment.

supply_chain.dependencies: Raw potatoes sourced primarily from Washington, Idaho, Oregon (US), Netherlands, Austria, Belgium, Germany, France, UK (Europe), Canada, China, Australia, Argentina. supply_chain.risks: Potato crop performance dependent on environmental and climate conditions. Severe weather (extreme heat, cold, heavy rainfall, flooding) can significantly affect potato crop yield and quality. Potatoes susceptible to pests and diseases. Concentration of ideal growing conditions in few geographic regions globally. Unfavorable crop conditions in one region can lead to significant demand on other regions. Supply chain disruptions from execution issues, extreme weather, natural disasters, fire, terrorism, pandemics, armed hostilities, strikes, labor shortages, cybersecurity breaches, governmental restrictions, supplier capacity constraints. Third-party supplier, co-packer, logistics provider performance risks.

supply_chain.risks: Products manufactured in foreign countries may cease to be available due to foreign government regulations, domestic government regulations, disruption in trade relationships and agreements, political unrest, war, disease, labor availability, or changes in local economic conditions. Shipment of goods from foreign countries could be delayed by container shipping companies encountering financial, capacity, or other difficulties. Trade policies could make sourcing product from overseas more difficult and/or more costly. Changes in trade policies, tariffs, product restrictions between Canada, U.S., and Mexico, or disruption in trading relationships can adversely impact business. Long supply chain lead times for products sourced from Asia create lag in ability to adjust inventory levels or costs in response to rapid changes in economic or cost conditions.

supply_chain.dependencies: The company relies heavily on third-party growers globally for seed production. Key raw materials include corn and soybean seeds, chlorinated pyridines derivatives, specialty intermediates, technical grade active ingredients, chlorine, and seed treatments. The company sources a material portion of crop protection inputs directly or indirectly from China. The company uses contract manufacturers both domestically and internationally to produce certain inputs or key components for product formulations.
supply_chain.risks: Volatility in input costs (raw materials, production costs) could significantly impact business and financial condition. Supply chain disruptions from geopolitical conflicts (Russia-Ukraine, potential China-Taiwan), cyberattacks, pandemics, weather events, or natural disasters could seriously harm operations. Concentration risk exists as a material portion of crop protection inputs are sourced from China. Dual- or multi-source supply chain strategies may not be adequate to address scope of disruptions from major conflicts. Business disruptions may be caused by security breaches including ransomware attacks. The company faces risks from industrial espionage and cyberattacks targeting confidential business information and trade secrets.

supply_chain.dependencies: The nicotine used in our operating companies' innovative smoke-free products is extracted from tobacco produced in one country. Single-source supplier risk for nicotine extract. Concentration risk in China for e-vapor product manufacturing (NJOY contracts with third-party importers). Wood tips used in cigar products and aluminum used in packaging subject to supply chain disruptions. Tobacco leaf varieties only available in limited geographies pose additional risks to price, availability and quality.
supply_chain.risks: Shifts in crops driven by macroeconomic conditions and adverse weather patterns. Government restrictions and mandated prices. Production control programs. Economic trade sanctions. Import duties and tariffs. International trade disruptions. Labor disruptions. Inflation. Geopolitical instability. Climate and environmental changes. Disruptions due to man-made or natural disasters. Reduced demand for tobacco leaf may result in reduced supply and availability of domestic tobacco and increased costs. Macroeconomic factors such as tariffs may exacerbate reductions in demand for tobacco leaf. Unavailability of nicotine extract necessary to manufacture products. Current geopolitical and macroeconomic conditions causing worldwide disruptions and delays to supply chains. Limited access to and increased cost of raw materials, ingredients and component parts. Reduced demand for raw materials may reduce supply and availability. Financial or operational difficulties of suppliers, distributors, logistics providers or third-party partners. Government taxes, restrictions and prohibitions on sale and use of certain products. Single-use plastic bans and extended producer responsibility mandates. Reliance on a few significant facilities and a small number of key suppliers, distributors and distribution chain service providers. Natural or man-made disaster, cybersecurity incident, global pandemic, labor disruption or other disruption affecting manufacturing operations or key suppliers.

supply_chain.dependencies: Fiber is the largest raw material cost to manufacture containerboard. The Company consumes both virgin wood fiber and recycled fiber in containerboard mills. All mills can utilize virgin wood fiber and all mills, other than the Valdosta mill, can utilize some recycled fiber. The Company procures wood fiber through leases of cutting rights, long-term supply agreements, and market purchases.
supply_chain.risks: The market price of wood fiber varies based upon availability, source, and the costs of fuels used in the harvesting and transportation of wood fiber. The cost and availability of wood fiber can be impacted by weather, general logging conditions, geography, and regulatory activity. The availability and cost of recycled fiber depends heavily on recycling rates and the domestic and global supply and demand for recycled products. Periods of higher recycled fiber costs and unusual price volatility have occurred in the past, including during 2024. Reduced truck and rail availability could lead to higher costs or poorer service. The Company has at times experienced lower availability of third-party trucking services, including truck and driver shortages, and service issues, interruptions, and delays in rail services.

supply_chain.dependencies: The Company relies on global supply chains and production and distribution processes that are complex and subject to increasing regulatory requirements. Raw materials essential to the Company's business are generally readily available from multiple sources.
supply_chain.risks: Interruptions and delays in manufacturing operations could adversely affect the Company's business, sales and reputation. Manufacturing disruptions can occur for many reasons including regulatory action, production quality deviations or safety issues, labor disputes, labor shortages, site-specific incidents (such as fires), natural disasters such as hurricanes and other severe weather events, raw material shortages, lack of available inspectors, political unrest, terrorist attacks and epidemics or pandemics. The Company relies on third parties to manufacture and supply certain of our products. Any failure by or loss of a third-party manufacturer or supplier could result in delays and increased costs.

data_points.company_type: Electronics Manufacturing Services and Diversified Manufacturing Services

supply_chain.dependencies: Some products require components available only from single sources. Component shortages occur from time to time. Certain sub-tier suppliers providing raw materials such as palladium, neon gas, and high-grade aluminum are partially dependent on supply from regions impacted by Russia/Ukraine conflict. Some parts sourced from suppliers located in Israel.
supply_chain.risks: Component shortages and supply chain disruptions due to industry-wide conditions, pandemics, natural disasters, and global events. Single-source supplier dependencies. Supplier financial condition affecting ability to supply components and satisfy warranty obligations. Geopolitical tensions (Russia/Ukraine conflict, Israel-Hamas war) potentially impacting supply chain. Inflation and rising component prices. Quality, reliability, or availability issues with component suppliers.

data_points.company_type: Off-Price Retail Apparel and Home Fashion

supply_chain.dependencies: A predominant portion of the apparel and other goods sold is originally manufactured in other countries, predominantly in Asia (including China). The Company directly sources a portion of products from foreign vendors and also buys products that originate from foreign sources indirectly through domestic vendors.
supply_chain.risks: Constraints on the availability of shipping capacity, changes in transportation or tariff costs, trade relationships or tax policies, geopolitical conflicts, natural disasters, or public health issues that reduce the supply or increase the relative cost of imported goods could result in disruptions to supply relationships.
